L1.area: Client-side content sanitization and dynamic DOM injection logic

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where the content sanitization logic fails to remove all dangerous event handler attributes and JavaScript URL schemes before injecting externally loaded SVG content into the DOM. The sanitization relies on an incomplete enumeration of event names, allowing attacker-controlled SVG files to execute arbitrary JavaScript when rendered in the browser.

L3.area: SVG content sanitization logic for event attribute filtering and URL scheme validation
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where the SVG sanitization logic uses an incomplete method to enumerate dangerous event handler attributes, missing SVG-specific animation events and certain non-standard browser events. Additionally, the sanitization fails to check all attributes that can contain JavaScript URL schemes. An attacker can craft a malicious SVG file that bypasses these sanitization checks to execute arbitrary JavaScript when the SVG is rendered in the browser.

ground_truth.reason: The SVG sanitization logic in svg-loader uses an incomplete list of DOM event names (missing SVG animation events like onbegin, onend, onrepeat and non-standard events) and fails to filter javascript: URLs in all relevant attributes, allowing crafted SVG files to execute arbitrary JavaScript.
